texto: Every time i see a butterfly, it reminds me of how precious life can truly be. To be able to turn from a caterpillar into a beautiful butterfly and fly away so freely and gracefully wherever she may please, without no one in the world to tell her what to do. I wait for that special moment in time when I get to live freely, without no worries, pain or tears. I just want to be happy. I want the laughter in the air without all of the pain. One special day I’ll get to live my life just like that beautiful butterfly. I will no longer feel blue inside.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michelle Knight: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Every time i see a butterfly, it reminds me of how precious life can truly be. To be able to turn…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, biography, emotional-pain pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de inspiracao e emotional-pain.
